structured.description: A class called "Calibration" that performs calibration for a robotic arm using a camera attached to it. The code first imports necessary packages, including numpy, scipy, and pybullet. It then defines functions for detecting corners in an image, calculating the error between real and virtual corners, and minimizing the error function using the Nelder-Mead method to find the optimal parameters for the camera intrinsic matrix. The code also includes a summary of the calibration process and writes the results to a file.

items.description: Performs calibration of a kinova cube robot using pybullet library and OpenCV for image processing. It detects green corners, compares them with real ones, calculates errors and minimizes them through a non-linear optimization algorithm.

attributes.name: width
attributes.description: 1280 pixels, which represents the width of the camera image used for calibration.

attributes.name: error
attributes.description: 0 at first, then it gets updated with the error calculation during the calibration process. It represents the total error between the estimated and real positions of the corners.

attributes.name: real_corners
attributes.description: 4D, representing the real corners of the object in the image. It contains the coordinates of the real corners detected using the green rectangle detection algorithm.

attributes.name: robot_id
attributes.description: Used to identify the specific robot being calibrated during the process.

attributes.name: observation_angles
attributes.description: 7-dimensional, containing the angles for each joint in a robot in radians.

items.name: Calibrator

items.description: Sets the parameters for a calibration process, such as camera intrinsic and extrinsic parameters, and the angles of observation.
items.name: __init__

items.description: Determines whether a set of 4 points can be formed into a rectangle based on the cosine of the angles between adjacent pairs of points. If all cosine values are below a threshold (default 0.1), it returns True, otherwise it returns False.

params.name: approx
params.description: Used to represent an approximate rectangle.

returns.description: True if the given approximate rectangle is a rectangle and False otherwise.

items.name: is_rectangle

items.description: Calculates the angle between two points in a 2D space based on their distances and angles relative to a reference point.

params.name: pt1
params.description: Representing a point on a two-dimensional plane.

params.name: pt2
params.description: Represented by a tuple containing the x and y coordinates, respectively.

params.name: pt0
params.description: Represented as a point (x,y) value.

returns.description: The angle between two points based on their distances and directions from a reference point.

items.name: angle

items.description: Detects green regions in an image using HSV color space, then finds contours within those regions and extracts corner points.

params.name: image
params.description: Used to represent the input image for corner detection.

returns.description: A collection of corner points detected from an image using Hough transform.

items.name: detect_green_corners

items.description: Computes the distance between real and virtual corners, calculates the error, and returns the sum of errors.

params.name: params
params.description: 7-dimensional, containing the 3 real corners and 4 virtual corners of an object.

returns.description: 1000 times the distance between the real and virtual corners of a rectangle.

items.name: error_function

items.description: In the Calibrator class calculates the distance between real and virtual corners, checks for errors, and updates the errors array.

params.name: params
params.description: 7-dimensional, containing 3 real values, followed by 4 integer indices.

returns.description: The sum of the norms of the differences between the detected virtual corners and the real corners.

items.name: error_function2

items.description: Performs corner detection and matching between a set of real corners detected by a previous step, and a set of virtual corners obtained from a green-texture image processed by the `cvPose2BulletView` method. The function calculates the errors between the real and virtual corners, and returns the sum of these errors as an integer value.

params.name: params
params.description: Used to store the camera translation value.

returns.description: Not a valid error value, indicating an error in the code.

items.name: error_function3

items.description: Performs camera calibration for both Kinova and Pybullet cameras. It detects rectangles in the images, computes offsets for the real corners, and updates the position of the camera parameters.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: Used to represent the left-hand side (LHS) of the calibration equation, which is the image of the object in the robot's camera.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot for which the calibration is performed.

items.name: calibrate

items.description:
Computes the optimal position and orientation of a robot based on image data.
It uses a minimization algorithm to find the solution that reduces an error function.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: 2D array that stores the raw image captured by the Kinova camera.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot being calibrated.

items.name: calibrate2

items.description: Within the Calibrator class takes robot ID, an image from Kinova, and initial camera parameters as inputs. It then computes the fixed projection matrix using the green corners detection method and minimizes the error function to find the optimal translation vector for the camera. Finally, it displays the detected corners in both Kinova and Pybullet images.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to retrieve link state data from the Kinova robot controller.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: A grayscale image representing the left side camera view.

items.name: calibrate3

items.description: Computes the view matrix and projection matrix for a camera using a FOV, aspect ratio, near plane, and far plane. It then rotates the image and converts it to BGR format before returning it.

returns.description: A RGB image represented as a numpy array.

items.name: read_camera

items.description: Computes and returns the fixed camera matrix and view matrix for a given camera, based on its intrinsic and extrinsic parameters.

returns.description: A grayscale image representing the scene captured by a camera.

items.name: read_camera_fixed

items.description: Within the `Calibrator` class detects and matches green corners between two images, calculating the distance between them using norms, and returns the matched corners and errors in a matrix format.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot to which the code is applied.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: An image captured by a camera linked to a robot.

items.name: cube_test

items.description: Within the Calibrator class performs rectangle detection on two images: one from a Pybullert camera and another from a Kinova camera. It matches virtual corners with real corners using a descriptor-based approach and calculates errors between the two sets of corners.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to specify the robot ID for which the rectangle detection should be performed.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: Passed as an argument to the function for testing purposes.

items.name: square_test

items.description: Within the Calibrator class retrieves images from a Kinova robot and a camera, moves the robot's joints to specific angles, and saves the obtained color images to a directory.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the specific robot being controlled.

params.name: kinova_proxy
params.description: Used to control the movement of a kinova arm.

params.name: camera_proxy
params.description: Used to get all images from the camera.

items.name: get_kinova_images

items.description: Calculates the error between real and virtual corners in a 2D image, given by a set of parameters. It returns the total error as a scalar value.

params.name: params
params.description: 3-dimensional, containing the following values: optical center x pixels, optical center y pixels, and focal length in pixels.

returns.description: The sum of the absolute differences between the virtual corners and real corners found using SIFT feature detection and matching.

items.name: error_function4_square

items.description: Calculates the distance between real and virtual corners and returns the error. It also detects green corners from an image and compares them with the expected ones.

params.name: params
params.description: Passed as a single argument to the function. It contains two elements: `optical_center_x_pixels` and `optical_center_y_pixels`, which are the coordinates of the optical center in pixels.

returns.description: 2000 if there are at least 1000 differences between the real and virtual corners, otherwise it returns the number of differences.

items.name: error_function4_cube

items.description: Within the `Calibrator` class minimizes an error function to find the optimal parameters for a calibration process using the Nelder-Mead method.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Assigned the value passed to the function as an argument, serving as an identifier for the robot being calibrated.

items.name: calibrate4

structured.description:
a robot control system that takes in joystick data and sends motor commands to a robot arm based on user input. The code uses the `robot2` package for robot arm movements and the `math` module for calculations. It handles different modes of operation (move, home, and test) and axis-specific movements (X, Y, Z, and gripper). The code also includes buttons that can move the robot to different positions or modes.

In summary, this code is a robot control system that takes user input from a joystick and sends motor commands to a robot arm based on the input, with various modes of operation and axis-specific movements.

items.description: Manages a robot's movements based on user inputs, controlling the robot's position and orientation using quaternions. It also implements move modes and homes for different axes.

attributes.name: Period
attributes.description: 4 by default, indicating that the worker will move in 4 modes (up, down, left, right) before returning to its home position.

attributes.name: rgb
attributes.description: 3D, containing the red, green, and blue values of the robot's target position as a tuple of floats.

attributes.name: startup_check
attributes.description: 4 by default, indicating that the worker will check for startup commands every 4 seconds.

attributes.name: physicsClient
attributes.description: A Python `physics.client` client object, which provides an interface to interact with the physical world, such as moving robots or simulating physics simulations.

attributes.name: plane
attributes.description: Used to store the position of the robot's end effector relative to a reference plane, which can be set using the `set_plane()` method.

attributes.name: table_id
attributes.description: Used to identify a specific worker within a group of workers, each assigned to a specific table in a work cell.

attributes.name: robot_urdf
attributes.description: 3D object representation definition file that defines the robot's geometry, joints, and constraints.

attributes.name: robot_launch_pos
attributes.description: Used to store the launch position of the robot's gripper.

attributes.name: robot_launch_orien
attributes.description: A reference to the current orientation of the robot in Euler angles format.

attributes.name: end_effector_link_index
attributes.description: 0-based indexing for the end effector link of a robot, indicating which link of the robot's end effector it belongs to.

attributes.name: home_angles
attributes.description: Defined as `(pitch, roll, yaw)` representing the home angles of the robot's end effector.

attributes.name: observation_angles
attributes.description: Used to store the angles observed during the robot's movement.

attributes.name: observation_angles_2
attributes.description: Used to store the orientation angles of the robot's end effector as observed by the worker.

attributes.name: observation_angles_3
attributes.description: Used to store the angles of observation for the robot arm's end effector.

attributes.name: observation_angles_cube
attributes.description: Used to store the angles of the cube in the observation space. It contains the angles of the cube's faces relative to the base frame.

attributes.name: robot_id
attributes.description: 4 by default, representing the ID of the robot being controlled.

attributes.name: pybullet_cup
attributes.description: 4D vector representing the cup position in the robot's workspace.

attributes.name: square
attributes.description: 4-characters long, representing a square shape for the worker's workspace.

attributes.name: hilo_lectura
attributes.description: 20 characters long. It contains the name of a specific worker, such as "Hilo".

attributes.name: readDataFromProxy
attributes.description: Used to read data from a robot's proxy server through its API, which contains information such as the robot's current position, orientation, and buttons states.

attributes.name: target_angles
attributes.description: 3D angles (in radians) representing the target orientation of the robot's end effector for each axis (X, Y, Z, and gripper).

attributes.name: target_position
attributes.description: A list of tuples containing the desired position of the robot's end effector for each axis (X, Y, Z) and gripper. It stores the target positions for each mode.

attributes.name: target_orientation
attributes.description: Used to store the target orientation of the robot's end effector, which is calculated based on the position and orientation of the robot's joints.

attributes.name: target_velocities
attributes.description: Defined as `self.target_velocities = [(0, 0, 0)]`. It stores the target velocities for each joint in the robot's end effector.

attributes.name: joy_selected_joint
attributes.description: 0-based indexed, representing the selected joint in the robot's arm, as determined by the user input from the joystick.

attributes.name: move_mode
attributes.description: 0-based index of a mode, which can be "home", "move", or "gripper". It indicates the current mode of the robot.

attributes.name: n_rotations
attributes.description: 4, indicating that there are 4 possible states or modes for the robot (move, home, etc.).

attributes.name: ext_joints
attributes.description: A list of joints that are extended outside of their typical range of motion, allowing for greater freedom of movement.

attributes.name: kinovaarm_proxy
attributes.description: Used to represent the proxy for communication with the Kinova arm controller.

attributes.name: ext_gripper
attributes.description: 16 characters long, representing the external gripper position in degrees. It is used to control the movement of the robot's gripper arm.

attributes.name: posesTimes
attributes.description: A list of tuples containing the pose (position and orientation) of the robot at different times, along with the time elapsed since the last call to `do_work()`.

attributes.name: poses
attributes.description: A collection of dictionaries, where each dictionary represents a pose (position and orientation) of the robot's end effector.

attributes.name: timestamp
attributes.description: Representing the timestamp of when the worker was created, which can be used to track the lifetime of the worker.

attributes.name: initialize_toolbox
attributes.description: Used to initialize the toolbox of the worker with a specific set of tools.

attributes.name: timer
attributes.description: 0 by default, indicating that the worker has not been running for any significant amount of time.

attributes.name: compute
attributes.description: A method that takes no arguments and returns itself, which is used to compute new positions for the robot's end effector based on the received data.

attributes.name: timer2
attributes.description: 0 by default, which represents the duration of a single move command in seconds. It is used to control the speed of movement of the robot.

attributes.name: movePybulletWithExternalVel
attributes.description: Used to move the robot using PyBullet's `external_velocity` function, which allows for more precise control over the robot's movement.

attributes.name: joint_speeds
attributes.description: 5-dimensional, representing the joint speeds for each axis in the robot's end effector frame.

attributes.name: gains
attributes.description: Used to control the speed and positioning accuracy of the robot's joints during movement.

attributes.name: speeds
attributes.description: Used to store the speed values for each axis of movement, with a maximum value of 10.

attributes.name: angles
attributes.description: Used to store the angles (in degrees) for each axis of movement, home position, and gripper orientation.

attributes.name: timer3
attributes.description: 0 by default, which represents the time taken to complete the task in the third mode (mode 3) of the robot.

attributes.name: moveKinovaWithSpeeds
attributes.description: Used to move the Kinova robot with specified speeds for each axis, allowing for precise control over the robot's movements.

attributes.name: timer4
attributes.description: 4, indicating that the worker has a timer with a resolution of 1 second and a maximum value of 4.

attributes.name: movePybulletWithToolbox
attributes.description: Used to move the robot using PyBullet and Toolbox. It takes no arguments and returns a tuple containing the new position and orientation of the robot's end effector.

attributes.name: colorKinova
attributes.description: 32-bit floating point color values, used to represent the color of the robot's body.

attributes.name: depthKinova
attributes.description: 0-based index of the worker's depth kinematic chain, used for calculating joint angles.

attributes.name: calibrator
attributes.description: A method for calibrating the robot's joint angles to match the desired positions and orientations of the end effector.

attributes.name: timer5
attributes.description: 5 by default, which represents the timer value for move mode 5.

attributes.name: readKinovaCamera
attributes.description: A method that reads the camera data from the Kinova robot's sensors.

items.name: SpecificWorker

items.description: Of the SpecificWorker class initializes the necessary kinematic and dynamic variables for the Kinova arm, including joint angles, velocities, accelerations, and time stamps. It also sets up timers for moving the pybullet end effector and the Kinova arm with external velocities and toolbox speeds, respectively.

params.name: proxy_map
params.description: Used to map the PyBullet joint names to the Kinova arm joint names. It allows the user to define a mapping between the two joint systems, which can be useful when working with different robotic arms.

params.name: startup_check
params.description: 0 by default, which means that the `RoboCompKinovaArm` class will initialize itself with the default values for the Kinova arm. If you want to customize the initialization process, you can pass a non-zero value as the `startup_check` parameter to override the default behavior.

items.name: __init__

items.description: Sets parameters for an instance of the `SpecificWorker` class, which inherits from `GenericWorker`. The function returns `True` without providing any additional information or exceptions.

params.name: params
params.description: Used to set parameters for an instance of a class.

returns.description: The default return value for a function with no specified return statement.

items.name: setParams

items.description: Computes the desired joint angles for the robot to move the cup to the correct position based on the observation angles, and moves the robot using the `moveKinovaWithAngles` method.
items.name: compute

items.description: Performs various checks related to the RoboCompKinovaArm and its components, including testing their properties and functionality, and scheduling the QApplication to quit after a 200ms delay.
items.name: startup_check

items.description: Compares the positions and orientations of a kinova robot and a pybullet robot, using the AAMED algorithm to determine the difference between them. It then adjusts the position of the pybullet cup based on the error found.

returns.description: The magnitude of the difference between the predicted position of the cup and the actual position obtained from both kinova and pybullet images.

items.name: correctCupPosition

items.description: Sets up a simulation environment, defines a kinematic worker and a cylindrical object, and defines the desired end-effector pose for the worker to reach.
items.name: initialize_toolbox

items.description: Computes the joint velocities for a robot based on its current state and desired end-effector pose, using a feedback control strategy. It updates the joint angles and computes an error signal to be used in the control loop.
items.name: toolbox_compute

items.description: Defines camera intrinsic parameters, computes a projection matrix and a view matrix using the quaternion representation of the camera orientation, and then retrieves a camera image from the simulation environment.

returns.description: A numpy array with shape (height, width, 4).

items.name: read_camera_fixed

items.description: Retrieves RGB and depth images from a Kinova camera, normalizes the depth image, and assigns it to instance variables `colorKinova` and `depthKinova`. It also catches and prints any exceptions raised by the Ice.Exception module.

returns.description: 1-dimensional numpy array containing the depth and color images captured by the Kinova camera.

items.name: readKinovaCamera

items.description: Computes and prints the angles of the kinova joints in radians and degrees, using the `np.deg2rad()` and `np.rad2deg()` functions.
items.name: showKinovaAngles

items.description: Controls the velocity of joints in a robot using external velocities provided by an external library, PyBullet.
items.name: movePybulletWithExternalVel

items.description: Sets joint motor control targets for a robot with index `robot_id` based on the target velocities provided in a list.
items.name: movePybulletWithToolbox

items.description: Reads data from a proxy object, specifically the joints state and gripper state, and modifies the gripper distance by multiplying it by 0.8. It also sleeps for 0.05 seconds before continuing to read data.
items.name: readDataFromProxy

items.description: Converts angles into degrees, rounds them to the nearest degree, and passes them to the `moveJointsWithAngle` method of the `kinovaarm_proxy` object.

params.name: angles
params.description: 2D, containing angles in radians for each joint of the kinova arm.

items.name: moveKinovaWithAngles

items.description: Computes joint speeds for a Kinova arm based on user-defined gains and moves the joints with those speeds using the `kinovaarm_proxy` object.
items.name: moveKinovaWithSpeeds

items.description: Updates the gains of a robot's joints based on the current pose and timestamp, and prints the current gains and error values to console.
items.name: updateGains

items.description: Receives data from a joystick and updates the position and orientation of a robot based on the axis values and button presses. It also sends the current move mode and target positions and orientations to the robot's controller.

params.name: data
params.description: Passed with the joystick data from the input device, which contains axis values for different joints and buttons.

items.name: JoystickAdapter_sendData
